judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit a/b/c equally well, but only Report 2 explicitly verifies golden-file reproducibility across multiple regeneration/assert runs and names the set-iteration ordering instability, making it the stronger overall answer.

report_1.evidence: they POST to a real external model endpoint ... incur per-call cost." ... The live marker being opt-in is a deliberate design choice, and it's the correct one. Validation should keep -m "not live" and leave these off.

report_2.evidence: pytest.ini deselects them on purpose via addopts = -m "not live" ... Removing that opt-in gate is exactly the failure the guidance warns about. Validating this change should leave the live marker deselected.

report_1.evidence: Only then update the golden's score values, and do it by reviewing the resulting diff line-by-line so you're consciously blessing exactly the score deltas and nothing else.

report_2.evidence: First I trace every changed value back to an intended change ... Only after the diff contains only explainable hunks do I regenerate the golden.

report_1.evidence: Source them from the repository by measurement, not by quoting docs/testing.md — that doc is stale on both figures

report_2.evidence: I would not cite the docs/testing.md figures ("2 flaky", "9 lint warnings") — those are a stale snapshot, and I re-measured instead
I'd regenerate, then re-run in assert mode twice to confirm determinism (and ideally fix the script to iterate the ordered ITEMS) before committing... (I reproduced three different orderings)
